calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-08-2004 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2004 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for settlement conference this day.
Commissioner Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR. Dave Davenport.
Brenda Cruz Keith, Esq. for Plaintiff Hayes Valley Inn., LLC. Represented by the property manager: R. Erickson.
Defendant, Peter Rauch present in pro per.
Parties have settled, terms read into the record as follows:
Stipulation for entry of judgment or dismissal with prejudice.
Plaintiff entitled to restitution and possession of the premises located at 417 Gough #414A aka 319 Ivy St., #414A, San Francisco, CA.
Defendant shall vacate under broom clean condition, return all keys to the building office on or before 5 p.m. June 15, 2004. Plaintiff will waive all unpaid rent and daily rental damages through May 15, 2004.
Each to bear own costs and fees, any property remaining on the premises will be disposed of The desk, chair and ceiling fan to be left in the room. Rental agreement is deemed forfeited.
If defendant fail to timely vacate, plaintiff may appear Ex Parte for Judgment pursuant to stipulation for possession of the premises, forfeiture of the rental agreement, back rent owing, daily damages, fees and costs. No futher stays, appeals, or motions for relief from forfeiture. Parties mutually release each other from all claims.
On or before 5 p.m. of May 10, 2004, defendant shall pay $550.00 to plaintiff's agent, representing one month rent for May 15 through June 14, 2004. If such payment is not received by that date, plaintiff is entitled to an Ex Parte application for Judgment.
Parties agreed and signed the stipulation in open court.
Matter is deemed settled and trial date vacated.
